title: ipomoea setosa subsp. melanotricha
intro: Ipomoea setosa subsp. melanotricha, also known as black-bearded morning glory, is a plant species in the Convolvulaceae family, characterized by its annual or perennial vines with heart-shaped leaves and small, trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Ipomoea setosa subsp. melanotricha include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in infusions, teas, or decoctions, and its dried roots and leaves are often used as a powder or tincture.
